author: gaxanh
content: I noticed that transferring ether in intrinsic tx has a `TX_VALUE_COST = 4,244`, while transferring ether internally has an `ACCOUNT_WRITE = 8000`. This means the price for transferring ether is inconsistent, which seems illogical.

author: misilva73
content: Based on our benchmarks, to have an execution performance of 100Mgas/s, a eth transfer with value should be 21k and a eth transfer without value should be 15k. The diff (6k) is the added cost of sending value. I am not sure why is lower than the difference between a CALL with value and without value (which atm is 8k), but this is what we observe in benchmarks

author: misilva73
content: One possible reason for this is that given that eth transfers do other stuff besides IO (and their total cost is higher), we cannot fit as many IO operations as when we do this with CALLs. So the diff could come from the IO bottlenecks

author: garyrong
content: it's the sum of the regular-gas primitives. Checkout  Interactions with other EIPs section in 2780
